summary: St Stephen returns, Bertha rockets, rare Phil-led China Doll
blurb: The first set kicks off with a punchy Wang Dang Doodle and Jack Straw before building momentum into the second set where Saint Stephen finally reappears after nine years. A thunderous Bertha jam drives the pre‑drums run, and Phil Lesh steps up for lead vocals on China Doll, a seldom‑heard moment.
review: On October 11 1983 at Madison Square Garden the Dead delivered one of those mythic MSG nights that fans still talk about. After an electric opener that included Wang Dang Doodle, Jack Straw and Bird Song, the second set unfolded like a rocket: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segued into a soaring I Need A Miracle → Bertha → Jam → China Doll, then the long‑awaited Saint Stephen burst out of Space with a volcanic intensity that sent the house into a frenzy. The pre‑drums run was a relentless wall of rhythm, propelling Throwing Stones into a blistering instrumental where Jerry’s guitar was on fire. Highlights also included Phil’s rare lead vocal on China Doll, Brent’s synth textures that made the jam sections “nutty crazy,” and a soaring Bird Song that showcased the band’s tight chemistry.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Out of 28 recordings analyzed, 25 are positive, 2 mixed and only 1 negative – a overwhelming consensus that the performance is exceptional, especially the Saint Stephen return, Bertha jam, and rare Phil vocal on China Doll.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crystal‑clear mix, full band balance and no dropouts – captures the historic Saint Stephen moment perfectly.

key_highlights: Long‑awaited Saint Stephen returns after a nine‑year gap with volcanic intensity.
Bertha launches into an explosive pre‑drums run that fuels the second‑set momentum.
Phil Lesh’s rare lead vocal on China Doll adds a special, almost historic moment.
Jerry’s scorching Throwing Stones instrumental and soaring Bird Song showcase top‑tier guitar work.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers soaring leads, cosmic improvisation early on and a fiery Throwing Stones instrumental; vocal moments are occasionally ragged but powerful.
band_performance.Phil: Lays down a nimble bass groove that underpins the jams and steps up for lead vocals on China Doll (and is noted as punchy in the mix).
band_performance.Bob: Provides solid rhythm guitar backing throughout, supporting the high‑energy set without criticism.
band_performance.Brent: Adds texture with synth/harpsichord flourishes, especially on China Doll and jam sections; generally praised despite occasional tinny description.
band_performance.Mickey: Together with Bill drives the dynamic pre‑drums run and lively Space sections, described as "just rockin" and full of energy.
band_performance.Billy: Powerful drumming that propels the second set’s momentum, highlighted on the Bertha jam and pre‑drums build.
